title: Hugo Broos finalizes South Africa FIFA World Cup squad
original_text:
Goalkeeper Ronwen Williams set to captain Bafana as nation return to World Cup for first time since 2010.
South Africa coach Hugo Broos has named a final 26-man squad for the FIFA World Cup 2026™. Bafana are set to compete at the global finals for the first time since hosting the tournament in 2010.
Key figures in Broos’ squad include inspirational captain and goalkeeper Ronwen Williams, dynamic midfielder Teboho Mokoena and Burnley striker Lyle Foster. Williams and Mokoena are among eight Mamelodi Sundowns players in the selection. Orlando Pirates also boast eight representatives, including exciting forward Oswin Appollis.

Williams on 2010 memories and opening-game buzzWilliams on 2010 memories and opening-game buzz
Goalkeeper reflects on what hosting 2010 World Cup did for the nation and Bafana's return to the global stage.Goalkeeper reflects on what hosting 2010 World Cup did for the nation and Bafana's return to the global stage.
Broos’ side will contest the opening game of the tournament against co-hosts Mexico at Mexico City Stadium. The match is a repeat of the South Africa 2010 curtain-raiser. They will then travel to Atlanta to face Czechia before rounding off their Group A campaign against Korea Republic in Monterrey.

All squads are provisional until the final list of 26 players is announced by FIFA, following their submission by participating teams, on 2 June.
South Africa's World Cup 2026 fixtures and groupSouth Africa's World Cup 2026 fixtures and group
11 June: Mexico v South Africa - Mexico City Stadium
18 June: Czechia v South Africa - Atlanta Stadium
24 June: South Africa v Korea Republic - Estadio Monterrey
